facade_description: 28 St Andrew St presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. HCD note: Each property has value as a late-nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Second Empire and other Victorian architectural styles.
